--- /dev/null
+++ b/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode/lifecycle.go
@@ -0,0 +1,321 @@
+package claudecode
+
+import (
+	"context"
+	"encoding/json"
+	"errors"
+	"fmt"
+	"io"
+	"log/slog"
+	"os"
+	"strings"
+	"time"
+
+	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
+	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
+	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/textutil"
+	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/transcript"
+)
+
+// Compile-time interface assertions for new interfaces.
+var (
+	_ agent.TranscriptAnalyzer     = (*ClaudeCodeAgent)(nil)
+	_ agent.TranscriptPreparer     = (*ClaudeCodeAgent)(nil)
+	_ agent.TokenCalculator        = (*ClaudeCodeAgent)(nil)
+	_ agent.SubagentAwareExtractor = (*ClaudeCodeAgent)(nil)
+)
+
+// HookNames returns the hook verbs Claude Code supports.
+// This is the new Agent interface method; delegates to GetHookNames for backward compatibility.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) HookNames() []string {
+	return c.GetHookNames()
+}
+
+// ParseHookEvent translates a Claude Code hook into a normalized lifecycle Event.
+// Returns nil if the hook has no lifecycle significance.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	switch hookName {
+	case HookNameSessionStart:
+		return c.parseSessionStart(stdin)
+	case HookNameUserPromptSubmit:
+		return c.parseTurnStart(stdin)
+	case HookNameStop:
+		return c.parseTurnEnd(stdin)
+	case HookNameSessionEnd:
+		return c.parseSessionEnd(stdin)
+	case HookNamePreTask:
+		return c.parseSubagentStart(stdin)
+	case HookNamePostTask:
+		return c.parseSubagentEnd(stdin)
+	case HookNamePostTodo:
+		// PostTodo is Claude-specific; handled outside the generic dispatcher.
+		return nil, nil //nolint:nilnil // nil event = no lifecycle action
+	default:
+		return nil, nil //nolint:nilnil // Unknown hooks have no lifecycle action
+	}
+}
+
+// ReadTranscript reads the raw JSONL transcript bytes for a session.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error) {
+	data, err := os.ReadFile(sessionRef) //nolint:gosec // Path comes from agent hook input
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read transcript: %w", err)
+	}
+	return data, nil
+}
+
+// ExtractPrompts extracts user prompts from the transcript starting at the given line offset.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) ExtractPrompts(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) ([]string, error) {
+	lines, _, err := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(sessionRef, fromOffset)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	var prompts []string
+	for i := range lines {
+		if lines[i].Type != transcript.TypeUser {
+			continue
+		}
+		content := transcript.ExtractUserContent(lines[i].Message)
+		if content != "" {
+			prompts = append(prompts, textutil.StripIDEContextTags(content))
+		}
+	}
+	return prompts, nil
+}
+
+// ExtractSummary extracts the last assistant message as a session summary.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) ExtractSummary(sessionRef string) (string, error) {
+	data, err := os.ReadFile(sessionRef) //nolint:gosec // Path comes from agent hook input
+	if err != nil {
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read transcript: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	lines, parseErr := transcript.ParseFromBytes(data)
+	if parseErr != nil {
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript: %w", parseErr)
+	}
+
+	// Walk backward to find last assistant text block
+	for i := len(lines) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
+		if lines[i].Type != transcript.TypeAssistant {
+			continue
+		}
+		var msg transcript.AssistantMessage
+		if err := json.Unmarshal(lines[i].Message, &msg); err != nil {
+			continue
+		}
+		for _, block := range msg.Content {
+			if block.Type == transcript.ContentTypeText && block.Text != "" {
+				return block.Text, nil
+			}
+		}
+	}
+	return "", nil
+}
+
+// PrepareTranscript waits for Claude Code's async transcript flush to complete.
+// Claude writes a hook_progress sentinel entry after flushing all pending writes.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) PrepareTranscript(sessionRef string) error {
+	waitForTranscriptFlush(sessionRef, time.Now())
+	return nil
+}
+
+// CalculateTokenUsage computes token usage from the transcript starting at the given line offset.
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) CalculateTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) (*agent.TokenUsage, error) {
+	// Subagent transcripts live in <transcriptDir>/<sessionID>/subagents/
+	// but we don't have the sessionID here. The caller should pass the transcript path
+	// which may contain the session ID in its directory structure.
+	// For now, compute subagentsDir from the transcript path structure.
+	return CalculateTotalTokenUsage(sessionRef, fromOffset, "")
+}
+
+// --- Internal hook parsing functions ---
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseSessionStart(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[sessionInfoRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	return &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.SessionStart,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseTurnStart(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[userPromptSubmitRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	return &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.TurnStart,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		Prompt:     raw.Prompt,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseTurnEnd(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[sessionInfoRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	return &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.TurnEnd,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseSessionEnd(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[sessionInfoRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	return &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.SessionEnd,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseSubagentStart(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[taskHookInputRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	return &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.SubagentStart,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		ToolUseID:  raw.ToolUseID,
+		ToolInput:  raw.ToolInput,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}, nil
+}
+
+func (c *ClaudeCodeAgent) parseSubagentEnd(stdin io.Reader) (*agent.Event, error) {
+	raw, err := readAndParse[postToolHookInputRaw](stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, err
+	}
+	event := &agent.Event{
+		Type:       agent.SubagentEnd,
+		SessionID:  raw.SessionID,
+		SessionRef: raw.TranscriptPath,
+		ToolUseID:  raw.ToolUseID,
+		ToolInput:  raw.ToolInput,
+		Timestamp:  time.Now(),
+	}
+	if raw.ToolResponse.AgentID != "" {
+		event.SubagentID = raw.ToolResponse.AgentID
+	}
+	return event, nil
+}
+
+// readAndParse reads stdin and unmarshals JSON into the given type.
+func readAndParse[T any](stdin io.Reader) (*T, error) {
+	data, err := io.ReadAll(stdin)
+	if err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to read hook input: %w", err)
+	}
+	if len(data) == 0 {
+		return nil, errors.New("empty hook input")
+	}
+	var result T
+	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &result); err != nil {
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
+	}
+	return &result, nil
+}
+
+// --- Transcript flush sentinel ---
+
+// stopHookSentinel is the string that appears in Claude Code's hook_progress
+// entry when the stop hook has been invoked, indicating the transcript is fully flushed.
+const stopHookSentinel = "hooks claude-code stop"
+
+// waitForTranscriptFlush polls the transcript file for the stop hook sentinel.
+// Falls back silently after a timeout.
+func waitForTranscriptFlush(transcriptPath string, hookStartTime time.Time) {
+	const (
+		maxWait      = 3 * time.Second
+		pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
+		tailBytes    = 4096
+		maxSkew      = 2 * time.Second
+	)
+
+	logCtx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "agent.claudecode")
+	deadline := time.Now().Add(maxWait)
+	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
+		if checkStopSentinel(transcriptPath, tailBytes, hookStartTime, maxSkew) {
+			logging.Debug(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel found",
+				slog.Duration("wait", time.Since(hookStartTime)),
+			)
+			return
+		}
+		time.Sleep(pollInterval)
+	}
+	logging.Warn(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel not found within timeout, proceeding",
+		slog.Duration("timeout", maxWait),
+	)
+}
+
+// checkStopSentinel reads the tail of the transcript file and looks for the sentinel.
+func checkStopSentinel(path string, tailBytes int64, hookStartTime time.Time, maxSkew time.Duration) bool {
+	f, err := os.Open(path) //nolint:gosec // path comes from agent hook input
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+	defer f.Close()
+
+	info, err := f.Stat()
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+	offset := info.Size() - tailBytes
+	if offset < 0 {
+		offset = 0
+	}
+	buf := make([]byte, info.Size()-offset)
+	if _, err := f.ReadAt(buf, offset); err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	lines := strings.Split(string(buf), "\n")
+	for _, line := range lines {
+		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
+		if line == "" || !strings.Contains(line, stopHookSentinel) {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		var entry struct {
+			Timestamp string `json:"timestamp"`
+		}
+		if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry) != nil || entry.Timestamp == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+		ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, entry.Timestamp)
+		if err != nil {
+			ts, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, entry.Timestamp)
+			if err != nil {
+				continue
+			}
+		}
+		// Validate timestamp is within acceptable range:
+		// - Not too far in the past (before hook started minus skew)
+		// - Not too far in the future (after hook started plus skew)
+		lowerBound := hookStartTime.Add(-maxSkew)
+		upperBound := hookStartTime.Add(maxSkew)
+		if ts.After(lowerBound) && ts.Before(upperBound) {
+			return true
+		}
+	}
+	return false
+}

+package claudecode

+
+func TestParseHookEvent_SessionStart(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	ag := &ClaudeCodeAgent{}
+	input := `{"session_id": "test-session-123", "transcript_path": "/tmp/transcript.jsonl"}`
+
+	event, err := ag.ParseHookEvent(HookNameSessionStart, strings.NewReader(input))
+
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
+	}
+	if event == nil {
+		t.Fatal("expected event, got nil")
+	}
+	if event.Type != agent.SessionStart {
+		t.Errorf("expected event type %v, got %v", agent.SessionStart, event.Type)
+	}
+	if event.SessionID != "test-session-123" {
+		t.Errorf("expected session_id 'test-session-123', got %q", event.SessionID)
+	}
+	if event.SessionRef != "/tmp/transcript.jsonl" {
+		t.Errorf("expected session_ref '/tmp/transcript.jsonl', got %q", event.SessionRef)
+	}
+	if event.Timestamp.IsZero() {
+		t.Error("expected non-zero timestamp")
+	}
+}
+
+func TestParseHookEvent_TurnStart(t *testing.T) {
+	t.Parallel()
+
+	ag := &ClaudeCodeAgent{}
+	input := `{"session_id": "sess-456", "transcript_path": "/tmp/t.jsonl", "prompt": "Hello world"}`
+
+	event, err := ag.ParseHookEvent(HookNameUserPromptSubmit, strings.NewReader(input))

+		t.Fatalf("unexpected error: %v", err)
+	}
+	if event == nil {
+		t.Fatal("expected event, got nil")
+	}
+	if event.Type != agent.TurnStart {
+		t.Errorf("expected event type %v, got %v", agent.TurnStart, event.Type)
+	}
+	if event.SessionID != "sess-456" {
+		t.Errorf("expected session_id 'sess-456', got %q", event.SessionID)
+	}
+	if event.Prompt != "Hello world" {
+		t.Errorf("expected prompt 'Hello world', got %q", event.Prompt)
+	}
+}

1→// hook_registry.go provides hook command registration for agents.
     2→// The lifecycle dispatcher (DispatchLifecycleEvent) handles all lifecycle events.
     3→// PostTodo is the only hook that's handled directly (not via lifecycle dispatcher).
     4→package cli
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"context"
     8→	"errors"
     9→	"fmt"
    10→	"log/slog"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    14→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/claudecode"
    15→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent/geminicli"
    16→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/logging"
    17→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/paths"
    18→
    19→	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    20→)
    21→
    22→// agentHookLogCleanup stores the cleanup function for agent hook logging.
    23→// Set by PersistentPreRunE, called by PersistentPostRunE.
    24→var agentHookLogCleanup func()
    25→
    26→// currentHookAgentName stores the agent name for the currently executing hook.
    27→// Set by newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging before calling the handler.
    28→// This allows handlers to know which agent invoked the hook without guessing.
    29→var currentHookAgentName agent.AgentName
    30→
    31→// GetCurrentHookAgent returns the agent for the currently executing hook.
    32→// Returns the agent based on the hook command structure (e.g., "entire hooks claude-code ...")
    33→// rather than guessing from directory presence.
    34→// Falls back to GetAgent() if not in a hook context.
    35→func GetCurrentHookAgent() (agent.Agent, error) {
    36→	if currentHookAgentName == "" {
    37→		return nil, errors.New("not in a hook context: agent name not set")
    38→	}
    39→
    40→	ag, err := agent.Get(currentHookAgentName)
    41→	if err != nil {
    42→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting hook agent %q: %w", currentHookAgentName, err)
    43→	}
    44→	return ag, nil
    45→}
    46→
    47→// newAgentHooksCmd creates a hooks subcommand for an agent that implements HookHandler.
    48→// It dynamically creates subcommands for each hook the agent supports.
    49→func newAgentHooksCmd(agentName agent.AgentName, handler agent.HookHandler) *cobra.Command {
    50→	cmd := &cobra.Command{
    51→		Use:    string(agentName),
    52→		Short:  handler.Description() + " hook handlers",
    53→		Hidden: true,
    54→		PersistentPreRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    55→			agentHookLogCleanup = initHookLogging()
    56→			return nil
    57→		},
    58→		PersistentPostRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    59→			if agentHookLogCleanup != nil {
    60→				agentHookLogCleanup()
    61→			}
    62→			return nil
    63→		},
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	for _, hookName := range handler.GetHookNames() {
    67→		cmd.AddCommand(newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName, hookName))
    68→	}
    69→
    70→	return cmd
    71→}
    72→
    73→// getHookType returns the hook type based on the hook name.
    74→// Returns "subagent" for task-related hooks (pre-task, post-task, post-todo),
    75→// "tool" for tool-related hooks (before-tool, after-tool),
    76→// "agent" for all other agent hooks.
    77→func getHookType(hookName string) string {
    78→	switch hookName {
    79→	case claudecode.HookNamePreTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTodo:
    80→		return "subagent"
    81→	case geminicli.HookNameBeforeTool, geminicli.HookNameAfterTool:
    82→		return "tool"
    83→	default:
    84→		return "agent"
    85→	}
    86→}
    87→
    88→// newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging creates a command for a specific hook verb with structured logging.
    89→// It uses the lifecycle dispatcher (ParseHookEvent → DispatchLifecycleEvent) as the primary path.
    90→// PostTodo is handled directly as it's Claude-specific and not part of the lifecycle dispatcher.
    91→func newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName agent.AgentName, hookName string) *cobra.Command {
    92→	return &cobra.Command{
    93→		Use:    hookName,
    94→		Hidden: true,
    95→		Short:  "Called on " + hookName,
    96→		RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
    97→			// Skip silently if not in a git repository - hooks shouldn't prevent the agent from working
    98→			if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
    99→				return nil
   100→			}
   101→
   102→			// Skip if Entire is not enabled
   103→			enabled, err := IsEnabled()
   104→			if err == nil && !enabled {
   105→				return nil
   106→			}
   107→
   108→			start := time.Now()
   109→
   110→			// Initialize logging context with agent name
   111→			ctx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), agentName)
   112→
   113→			// Get strategy name for logging
   114→			strategyName := unknownStrategyName //nolint:ineffassign,wastedassign // already present in codebase
   115→			strategyName = GetStrategy().Name()
   116→
   117→			hookType := getHookType(hookName)
   118→
   119→			logging.Debug(ctx, "hook invoked",
   120→				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   121→				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   122→				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   123→			)
   124→
   125→			// Set the current hook agent so handlers can retrieve it
   126→			currentHookAgentName = agentName
   127→			defer func() { currentHookAgentName = "" }()
   128→
   129→			// Use the lifecycle dispatcher for all hooks
   130→			var hookErr error
   131→			ag, agentErr := agent.Get(agentName)
   132→			if agentErr != nil {
   133→				return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent %q: %w", agentName, agentErr)
   134→			}
   135→
   136→			// Use cmd.InOrStdin() to support testing with cmd.SetIn()
   137→			event, parseErr := ag.ParseHookEvent(hookName, cmd.InOrStdin())
   138→			if parseErr != nil {
   139→				return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook event: %w", parseErr)
   140→			}
   141→
   142→			if event != nil {
   143→				// Lifecycle event — use the generic dispatcher
   144→				hookErr = DispatchLifecycleEvent(ag, event)
   145→			} else if agentName == agent.AgentNameClaudeCode && hookName == claudecode.HookNamePostTodo {
   146→				// PostTodo is Claude-specific: creates incremental checkpoints during subagent execution
   147→				hookErr = handleClaudeCodePostTodo()
   148→			}
   149→			// Other pass-through hooks (nil event, no special handling) are no-ops
   150→
   151→			logging.LogDuration(ctx, slog.LevelDebug, "hook completed", start,
   152→				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   153→				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   154→				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   155→				slog.Bool("success", hookErr == nil),
   156→			)
   157→
   158→			return hookErr
   159→		},
   160→	}
   161→}
   162→

1→// Package agent provides interfaces and types for integrating with coding agents.
     2→// It abstracts agent-specific behavior (hooks, log parsing, session storage) so that
     3→// the same Strategy implementations can work with any coding agent.
     4→package agent
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"io"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// Agent defines the interface for interacting with a coding agent.
    11→// Each agent implementation (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, etc.) converts its
    12→// native format to the normalized types defined in this package.
    13→//
    14→// The interface is organized into four groups:
    15→//
    16→//   - Identity (5 methods): Name, Type, Description, DetectPresence, ProtectedDirs
    17→//   - Event Mapping (2 methods): HookNames, ParseHookEvent
    18→//   - Transcript Storage (3 methods): ReadTranscript, ChunkTranscript, ReassembleTranscript
    19→//   - Legacy (8 methods): Will be moved to optional interfaces or removed in a future phase
    20→type Agent interface {
    21→	// --- Identity ---
    22→
    23→	// Name returns the agent registry key (e.g., "claude-code", "gemini")
    24→	Name() AgentName
    25→
    26→	// Type returns the agent type identifier (e.g., "Claude Code", "Gemini CLI")
    27→	// This is stored in metadata and trailers.
    28→	Type() AgentType
    29→
    30→	// Description returns a human-readable description for UI
    31→	Description() string
    32→
    33→	// DetectPresence checks if this agent is configured in the repository
    34→	DetectPresence() (bool, error)
    35→
    36→	// ProtectedDirs returns repo-root-relative directories that should never be
    37→	// modified or deleted during rewind or other destructive operations.
    38→	// Examples: [".claude"] for Claude, [".gemini"] for Gemini.
    39→	ProtectedDirs() []string
    40→
    41→	// --- Event Mapping ---
    42→
    43→	// HookNames returns the hook verbs this agent supports.
    44→	// These become subcommands under `entire hooks <agent>`.
    45→	// e.g., ["stop", "user-prompt-submit", "session-start", "session-end"]
    46→	HookNames() []string
    47→
    48→	// ParseHookEvent translates an agent-native hook into a normalized lifecycle Event.
    49→	// Returns nil if the hook has no lifecycle significance (e.g., pass-through hooks).
    50→	// This is the core contribution surface for new agent implementations.
    51→	ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*Event, error)
    52→
    53→	// --- Transcript Storage ---
    54→
    55→	// ReadTranscript reads the raw transcript bytes for a session.
    56→	ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error)
    57→
    58→	// ChunkTranscript splits a transcript into chunks if it exceeds maxSize.
    59→	// Returns a slice of chunks. If the transcript fits in one chunk, returns single-element slice.
    60→	// The chunking is format-aware: JSONL splits at line boundaries, JSON splits message arrays.
    61→	ChunkTranscript(content []byte, maxSize int) ([][]byte, error)
    62→
    63→	// ReassembleTranscript combines chunks back into a single transcript.
    64→	// Handles format-specific reassembly (JSONL concatenation, JSON message merging).
    65→	ReassembleTranscript(chunks [][]byte) ([]byte, error)
    66→
    67→	// --- Legacy methods (will move to optional interfaces in Phase 4) ---
    68→
    69→	// GetHookConfigPath returns path to hook config file (empty if none).
    70→	GetHookConfigPath() string
    71→
    72→	// SupportsHooks returns true if agent supports lifecycle hooks.
    73→	SupportsHooks() bool
    74→
    75→	// ParseHookInput parses hook callback input from stdin.
    76→	ParseHookInput(hookType HookType, reader io.Reader) (*HookInput, error)
    77→
    78→	// GetSessionID extracts session ID from hook input.
    79→	GetSessionID(input *HookInput) string
    80→
    81→	// GetSessionDir returns where agent stores session data for this repo.
    82→	GetSessionDir(repoPath string) (string, error)
    83→
    84→	// ResolveSessionFile returns the path to the session transcript file.
    85→	ResolveSessionFile(sessionDir, agentSessionID string) string
    86→
    87→	// ReadSession reads session data from agent's storage.
    88→	ReadSession(input *HookInput) (*AgentSession, error)
    89→
    90→	// WriteSession writes session data for resumption.
    91→	WriteSession(session *AgentSession) error
    92→
    93→	// FormatResumeCommand returns command to resume a session.
    94→	FormatResumeCommand(sessionID string) string
    95→}
    96→
    97→// HookSupport is implemented by agents with lifecycle hooks.
    98→// This optional interface allows agents like Claude Code and Cursor to
    99→// install and manage hooks that notify Entire of agent events.
   100→type HookSupport interface {
   101→	Agent
   102→
   103→	// InstallHooks installs agent-specific hooks.
   104→	// If localDev is true, hooks point to local development build.
   105→	// If force is true, removes existing Entire hooks before installing.
   106→	// Returns the number of hooks installed.
   107→	InstallHooks(localDev bool, force bool) (int, error)
   108→
   109→	// UninstallHooks removes installed hooks
   110→	UninstallHooks() error
   111→
   112→	// AreHooksInstalled checks if hooks are currently installed
   113→	AreHooksInstalled() bool
   114→
   115→	// GetSupportedHooks returns the hook types this agent supports
   116→	GetSupportedHooks() []HookType
   117→}
   118→
   119→// HookHandler is implemented by agents that define their own hook vocabulary.
   120→// HookNames() is now part of the core Agent interface.
   121→// This interface is kept for backward compatibility during migration.
   122→type HookHandler interface {
   123→	Agent
   124→
   125→	// GetHookNames returns the hook verbs this agent supports.
   126→	GetHookNames() []string
   127→}
   128→
   129→// FileWatcher is implemented by agents that use file-based detection.
   130→// Agents like Aider that don't support hooks can use file watching
   131→// to detect session activity.
   132→type FileWatcher interface {
   133→	Agent
   134→
   135→	// GetWatchPaths returns paths to watch for session changes
   136→	GetWatchPaths() ([]string, error)
   137→
   138→	// OnFileChange handles a detected file change and returns session info
   139→	OnFileChange(path string) (*SessionChange, error)
   140→}
   141→
   142→// TranscriptAnalyzer provides format-specific transcript parsing.
   143→// Agents that implement this get richer checkpoints (transcript-derived file lists,
   144→// prompts, summaries). Agents that don't still participate in the checkpoint lifecycle
   145→// via git-status-based file detection and raw transcript storage.
   146→type TranscriptAnalyzer interface {
   147→	Agent
   148→
   149→	// GetTranscriptPosition returns the current position (length) of a transcript.
   150→	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), this is the line count.
   151→	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), this is the message count.
   152→	// Returns 0 if the file doesn't exist or is empty.
   153→	GetTranscriptPosition(path string) (int, error)
   154→
   155→	// ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset extracts files modified since a given offset.
   156→	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), offset is the starting line number.
   157→	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), offset is the starting message index.
   158→	// Returns:
   159→	//   - files: list of file paths modified by the agent (from Write/Edit tools)
   160→	//   - currentPosition: the current position (line count or message count)
   161→	//   - error: any error encountered during reading
   162→	ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset(path string, startOffset int) (files []string, currentPosition int, err error)
   163→
   164→	// ExtractPrompts extracts user prompts from the transcript starting at the given offset.
   165→	ExtractPrompts(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) ([]string, error)
   166→
   167→	// ExtractSummary extracts a summary of the session from the transcript.
   168→	ExtractSummary(sessionRef string) (string, error)
   169→}
   170→
   171→// TranscriptPreparer is called before ReadTranscript to handle agent-specific
   172→// flush/sync requirements (e.g., Claude Code's async transcript writing).
   173→// The framework calls PrepareTranscript before ReadTranscript if implemented.
   174→type TranscriptPreparer interface {
   175→	Agent
   176→
   177→	// PrepareTranscript ensures the transcript is ready to read.
   178→	// For Claude Code, this waits for the async transcript flush to complete.
   179→	PrepareTranscript(sessionRef string) error
   180→}
   181→
   182→// TokenCalculator provides token usage calculation for a session.
   183→// The framework calls this during step save and checkpoint if implemented.
   184→type TokenCalculator interface {
   185→	Agent
   186→
   187→	// CalculateTokenUsage computes token usage from the transcript starting at the given offset.
   188→	CalculateTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) (*TokenUsage, error)
   189→}
   190→
   191→// SubagentAwareExtractor provides methods for extracting files and tokens including subagents.
   192→// Agents that support spawning subagents (like Claude Code's Task tool) should implement this
   193→// to ensure subagent contributions are included in checkpoints.
   194→type SubagentAwareExtractor interface {
   195→	Agent
   196→
   197→	// ExtractAllModifiedFiles extracts files modified by both the main agent and any spawned subagents.
   198→	// The subagentsDir parameter specifies where subagent transcripts are stored.
   199→	// Returns a deduplicated list of all modified file paths.
   200→	ExtractAllModifiedFiles(sessionRef string, fromOffset int, subagentsDir string) ([]string, error)
   201→
   202→	// CalculateTotalTokenUsage computes token usage including all spawned subagents.
   203→	// The subagentsDir parameter specifies where subagent transcripts are stored.
   204→	CalculateTotalTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int, subagentsDir string) (*TokenUsage, error)
   205→}
   206→

1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// EventType represents a normalized lifecycle event from any agent.
     9→// Agents translate their native hooks into these event types via ParseHookEvent.
    10→type EventType int
    11→
    12→const (
    13→	// SessionStart indicates the agent session has begun.
    14→	SessionStart EventType = iota + 1
    15→
    16→	// TurnStart indicates the user submitted a prompt and the agent is about to work.
    17→	TurnStart
    18→
    19→	// TurnEnd indicates the agent finished responding to a prompt.
    20→	TurnEnd
    21→
    22→	// Compaction indicates the agent is about to compress its context window.
    23→	// This triggers the same save logic as TurnEnd but also resets the transcript offset.
    24→	Compaction
    25→
    26→	// SessionEnd indicates the session has been terminated.
    27→	SessionEnd
    28→
    29→	// SubagentStart indicates a subagent (task) has been spawned.
    30→	SubagentStart
    31→
    32→	// SubagentEnd indicates a subagent (task) has completed.
    33→	SubagentEnd
    34→)
    35→
    36→// String returns a human-readable name for the event type.
    37→func (e EventType) String() string {
    38→	switch e {
    39→	case SessionStart:
    40→		return "SessionStart"
    41→	case TurnStart:
    42→		return "TurnStart"
    43→	case TurnEnd:
    44→		return "TurnEnd"
    45→	case Compaction:
    46→		return "Compaction"
    47→	case SessionEnd:
    48→		return "SessionEnd"
    49→	case SubagentStart:
    50→		return "SubagentStart"
    51→	case SubagentEnd:
    52→		return "SubagentEnd"
    53→	default:
    54→		return "Unknown"
    55→	}
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Event is a normalized lifecycle event produced by an agent's ParseHookEvent method.
    59→// The framework dispatcher uses these events to drive checkpoint/session lifecycle actions.
    60→type Event struct {
    61→	// Type is the kind of lifecycle event.
    62→	Type EventType
    63→
    64→	// SessionID identifies the agent session.
    65→	SessionID string
    66→
    67→	// PreviousSessionID is non-empty when this event represents a session continuation
    68→	// or handoff (e.g., Claude starting a new session ID after exiting plan mode).
    69→	PreviousSessionID string
    70→
    71→	// SessionRef is an agent-specific reference to the transcript (typically a file path).
    72→	SessionRef string
    73→
    74→	// Prompt is the user's prompt text (populated on TurnStart events).
    75→	Prompt string
    76→
    77→	// Timestamp is when the event occurred.
    78→	Timestamp time.Time
    79→
    80→	// ToolUseID identifies the tool invocation (for SubagentStart/SubagentEnd events).
    81→	ToolUseID string
    82→
    83→	// SubagentID identifies the subagent instance (for SubagentEnd events).
    84→	SubagentID string
    85→
    86→	// ToolInput is the raw tool input JSON (for subagent type/description extraction).
    87→	ToolInput json.RawMessage
    88→
    89→	// ResponseMessage is an optional message to display to the user via the agent.
    90→	ResponseMessage string
    91→
    92→	// Metadata holds agent-specific state that the framework stores and makes available
    93→	// on subsequent events. Examples: Pi's activeLeafId, Cursor's is_background_agent.
    94→	Metadata map[string]string
    95→}
    96→

1→// Package agent provides interfaces and types for integrating with coding agents.
     2→// It abstracts agent-specific behavior (hooks, log parsing, session storage) so that
     3→// the same Strategy implementations can work with any coding agent.
     4→package agent
     5→
     6→import (
     7→	"io"
     8→)
     9→
    10→// Agent defines the interface for interacting with a coding agent.
    11→// Each agent implementation (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, etc.) converts its
    12→// native format to the normalized types defined in this package.
    13→//
    14→// The interface is organized into four groups:
    15→//
    16→//   - Identity (5 methods): Name, Type, Description, DetectPresence, ProtectedDirs
    17→//   - Event Mapping (2 methods): HookNames, ParseHookEvent
    18→//   - Transcript Storage (3 methods): ReadTranscript, ChunkTranscript, ReassembleTranscript
    19→//   - Legacy (8 methods): Will be moved to optional interfaces or removed in a future phase
    20→type Agent interface {
    21→	// --- Identity ---
    22→
    23→	// Name returns the agent registry key (e.g., "claude-code", "gemini")
    24→	Name() AgentName
    25→
    26→	// Type returns the agent type identifier (e.g., "Claude Code", "Gemini CLI")
    27→	// This is stored in metadata and trailers.
    28→	Type() AgentType
    29→
    30→	// Description returns a human-readable description for UI
    31→	Description() string
    32→
    33→	// DetectPresence checks if this agent is configured in the repository
    34→	DetectPresence() (bool, error)
    35→
    36→	// ProtectedDirs returns repo-root-relative directories that should never be
    37→	// modified or deleted during rewind or other destructive operations.
    38→	// Examples: [".claude"] for Claude, [".gemini"] for Gemini.
    39→	ProtectedDirs() []string
    40→
    41→	// --- Event Mapping ---
    42→
    43→	// HookNames returns the hook verbs this agent supports.
    44→	// These become subcommands under `entire hooks <agent>`.
    45→	// e.g., ["stop", "user-prompt-submit", "session-start", "session-end"]
    46→	HookNames() []string
    47→
    48→	// ParseHookEvent translates an agent-native hook into a normalized lifecycle Event.
    49→	// Returns nil if the hook has no lifecycle significance (e.g., pass-through hooks).
    50→	// This is the core contribution surface for new agent implementations.
    51→	ParseHookEvent(hookName string, stdin io.Reader) (*Event, error)
    52→
    53→	// --- Transcript Storage ---
    54→
    55→	// ReadTranscript reads the raw transcript bytes for a session.
    56→	ReadTranscript(sessionRef string) ([]byte, error)
    57→
    58→	// ChunkTranscript splits a transcript into chunks if it exceeds maxSize.
    59→	// Returns a slice of chunks. If the transcript fits in one chunk, returns single-element slice.
    60→	// The chunking is format-aware: JSONL splits at line boundaries, JSON splits message arrays.
    61→	ChunkTranscript(content []byte, maxSize int) ([][]byte, error)
    62→
    63→	// ReassembleTranscript combines chunks back into a single transcript.
    64→	// Handles format-specific reassembly (JSONL concatenation, JSON message merging).
    65→	ReassembleTranscript(chunks [][]byte) ([]byte, error)
    66→
    67→	// --- Legacy methods (will move to optional interfaces in Phase 4) ---
    68→
    69→	// GetHookConfigPath returns path to hook config file (empty if none).
    70→	GetHookConfigPath() string
    71→
    72→	// SupportsHooks returns true if agent supports lifecycle hooks.
    73→	SupportsHooks() bool
    74→
    75→	// ParseHookInput parses hook callback input from stdin.
    76→	ParseHookInput(hookType HookType, reader io.Reader) (*HookInput, error)
    77→
    78→	// GetSessionID extracts session ID from hook input.
    79→	GetSessionID(input *HookInput) string
    80→
    81→	// GetSessionDir returns where agent stores session data for this repo.
    82→	GetSessionDir(repoPath string) (string, error)
    83→
    84→	// ResolveSessionFile returns the path to the session transcript file.
    85→	ResolveSessionFile(sessionDir, agentSessionID string) string
    86→
    87→	// ReadSession reads session data from agent's storage.
    88→	ReadSession(input *HookInput) (*AgentSession, error)
    89→
    90→	// WriteSession writes session data for resumption.
    91→	WriteSession(session *AgentSession) error
    92→
    93→	// FormatResumeCommand returns command to resume a session.
    94→	FormatResumeCommand(sessionID string) string
    95→}
    96→
    97→// HookSupport is implemented by agents with lifecycle hooks.
    98→// This optional interface allows agents like Claude Code and Cursor to
    99→// install and manage hooks that notify Entire of agent events.
   100→type HookSupport interface {
   101→	Agent
   102→
   103→	// InstallHooks installs agent-specific hooks.
   104→	// If localDev is true, hooks point to local development build.
   105→	// If force is true, removes existing Entire hooks before installing.
   106→	// Returns the number of hooks installed.
   107→	InstallHooks(localDev bool, force bool) (int, error)
   108→
   109→	// UninstallHooks removes installed hooks
   110→	UninstallHooks() error
   111→
   112→	// AreHooksInstalled checks if hooks are currently installed
   113→	AreHooksInstalled() bool
   114→
   115→	// GetSupportedHooks returns the hook types this agent supports
   116→	GetSupportedHooks() []HookType
   117→}
   118→
   119→// HookHandler is implemented by agents that define their own hook vocabulary.
   120→// HookNames() is now part of the core Agent interface.
   121→// This interface is kept for backward compatibility during migration.
   122→type HookHandler interface {
   123→	Agent
   124→
   125→	// GetHookNames returns the hook verbs this agent supports.
   126→	GetHookNames() []string
   127→}
   128→
   129→// FileWatcher is implemented by agents that use file-based detection.
   130→// Agents like Aider that don't support hooks can use file watching
   131→// to detect session activity.
   132→type FileWatcher interface {
   133→	Agent
   134→
   135→	// GetWatchPaths returns paths to watch for session changes
   136→	GetWatchPaths() ([]string, error)
   137→
   138→	// OnFileChange handles a detected file change and returns session info
   139→	OnFileChange(path string) (*SessionChange, error)
   140→}
   141→
   142→// TranscriptAnalyzer provides format-specific transcript parsing.
   143→// Agents that implement this get richer checkpoints (transcript-derived file lists,
   144→// prompts, summaries). Agents that don't still participate in the checkpoint lifecycle
   145→// via git-status-based file detection and raw transcript storage.
   146→type TranscriptAnalyzer interface {
   147→	Agent
   148→
   149→	// GetTranscriptPosition returns the current position (length) of a transcript.
   150→	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), this is the line count.
   151→	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), this is the message count.
   152→	// Returns 0 if the file doesn't exist or is empty.
   153→	GetTranscriptPosition(path string) (int, error)
   154→
   155→	// ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset extracts files modified since a given offset.
   156→	// For JSONL formats (Claude Code), offset is the starting line number.
   157→	// For JSON formats (Gemini CLI), offset is the starting message index.
   158→	// Returns:
   159→	//   - files: list of file paths modified by the agent (from Write/Edit tools)
   160→	//   - currentPosition: the current position (line count or message count)
   161→	//   - error: any error encountered during reading
   162→	ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset(path string, startOffset int) (files []string, currentPosition int, err error)
   163→
   164→	// ExtractPrompts extracts user prompts from the transcript starting at the given offset.
   165→	ExtractPrompts(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) ([]string, error)
   166→
   167→	// ExtractSummary extracts a summary of the session from the transcript.
   168→	ExtractSummary(sessionRef string) (string, error)
   169→}
   170→
   171→// TranscriptPreparer is called before ReadTranscript to handle agent-specific
   172→// flush/sync requirements (e.g., Claude Code's async transcript writing).
   173→// The framework calls PrepareTranscript before ReadTranscript if implemented.
   174→type TranscriptPreparer interface {
   175→	Agent
   176→
   177→	// PrepareTranscript ensures the transcript is ready to read.
   178→	// For Claude Code, this waits for the async transcript flush to complete.
   179→	PrepareTranscript(sessionRef string) error
   180→}
   181→
   182→// TokenCalculator provides token usage calculation for a session.
   183→// The framework calls this during step save and checkpoint if implemented.
   184→type TokenCalculator interface {
   185→	Agent
   186→
   187→	// CalculateTokenUsage computes token usage from the transcript starting at the given offset.
   188→	CalculateTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int) (*TokenUsage, error)
   189→}
   190→
   191→// SubagentAwareExtractor provides methods for extracting files and tokens including subagents.
   192→// Agents that support spawning subagents (like Claude Code's Task tool) should implement this
   193→// to ensure subagent contributions are included in checkpoints.
   194→type SubagentAwareExtractor interface {
   195→	Agent
   196→
   197→	// ExtractAllModifiedFiles extracts files modified by both the main agent and any spawned subagents.
   198→	// The subagentsDir parameter specifies where subagent transcripts are stored.
   199→	// Returns a deduplicated list of all modified file paths.
   200→	ExtractAllModifiedFiles(sessionRef string, fromOffset int, subagentsDir string) ([]string, error)
   201→
   202→	// CalculateTotalTokenUsage computes token usage including all spawned subagents.
   203→	// The subagentsDir parameter specifies where subagent transcripts are stored.
   204→	CalculateTotalTokenUsage(sessionRef string, fromOffset int, subagentsDir string) (*TokenUsage, error)
   205→}
   206→

1→package agent
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"encoding/json"
     5→	"time"
     6→)
     7→
     8→// EventType represents a normalized lifecycle event from any agent.
     9→// Agents translate their native hooks into these event types via ParseHookEvent.
    10→type EventType int
    11→
    12→const (
    13→	// SessionStart indicates the agent session has begun.
    14→	SessionStart EventType = iota + 1
    15→
    16→	// TurnStart indicates the user submitted a prompt and the agent is about to work.
    17→	TurnStart
    18→
    19→	// TurnEnd indicates the agent finished responding to a prompt.
    20→	TurnEnd
    21→
    22→	// Compaction indicates the agent is about to compress its context window.
    23→	// This triggers the same save logic as TurnEnd but also resets the transcript offset.
    24→	Compaction
    25→
    26→	// SessionEnd indicates the session has been terminated.
    27→	SessionEnd
    28→
    29→	// SubagentStart indicates a subagent (task) has been spawned.
    30→	SubagentStart
    31→
    32→	// SubagentEnd indicates a subagent (task) has completed.
    33→	SubagentEnd
    34→)
    35→
    36→// String returns a human-readable name for the event type.
    37→func (e EventType) String() string {
    38→	switch e {
    39→	case SessionStart:
    40→		return "SessionStart"
    41→	case TurnStart:
    42→		return "TurnStart"
    43→	case TurnEnd:
    44→		return "TurnEnd"
    45→	case Compaction:
    46→		return "Compaction"
    47→	case SessionEnd:
    48→		return "SessionEnd"
    49→	case SubagentStart:
    50→		return "SubagentStart"
    51→	case SubagentEnd:
    52→		return "SubagentEnd"
    53→	default:
    54→		return "Unknown"
    55→	}
    56→}
    57→
    58→// Event is a normalized lifecycle event produced by an agent's ParseHookEvent method.
    59→// The framework dispatcher uses these events to drive checkpoint/session lifecycle actions.
    60→type Event struct {
    61→	// Type is the kind of lifecycle event.
    62→	Type EventType
    63→
    64→	// SessionID identifies the agent session.
    65→	SessionID string
    66→
    67→	// PreviousSessionID is non-empty when this event represents a session continuation
    68→	// or handoff (e.g., Claude starting a new session ID after exiting plan mode).
    69→	PreviousSessionID string
    70→
    71→	// SessionRef is an agent-specific reference to the transcript (typically a file path).
    72→	SessionRef string
    73→
    74→	// Prompt is the user's prompt text (populated on TurnStart events).
    75→	Prompt string
    76→
    77→	// Timestamp is when the event occurred.
    78→	Timestamp time.Time
    79→
    80→	// ToolUseID identifies the tool invocation (for SubagentStart/SubagentEnd events).
    81→	ToolUseID string
    82→
    83→	// SubagentID identifies the subagent instance (for SubagentEnd events).
    84→	SubagentID string
    85→
    86→	// ToolInput is the raw tool input JSON (for subagent type/description extraction).
    87→	ToolInput json.RawMessage
    88→
    89→	// ResponseMessage is an optional message to display to the user via the agent.
    90→	ResponseMessage string
    91→
    92→	// Metadata holds agent-specific state that the framework stores and makes available
    93→	// on subsequent events. Examples: Pi's activeLeafId, Cursor's is_background_agent.
    94→	Metadata map[string]string
    95→}
    96→

1→// Package strategy provides an interface for different git strategies
     2→// that can be used to save and manage Claude Code session changes.
     3→package strategy
     4→
     5→import (
     6→	"encoding/json"
     7→	"errors"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"strings"
    10→	"time"
    11→
    12→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/agent"
    13→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/checkpoint/id"
    14→	"github.com/entireio/cli/cmd/entire/cli/session"
    15→)
    16→
    17→// ErrNoMetadata is returned when a commit does not have an Entire metadata trailer.
    18→var ErrNoMetadata = errors.New("commit has no entire metadata")
    19→
    20→// ErrNoSession is returned when no session info is available.
    21→var ErrNoSession = errors.New("no session info available")
    22→
    23→// ErrNotTaskCheckpoint is returned when a rewind point is not a task checkpoint.
    24→var ErrNotTaskCheckpoint = errors.New("not a task checkpoint")
    25→
    26→// ErrNotImplemented is returned when a feature is not yet implemented.
    27→var ErrNotImplemented = errors.New("not implemented")
    28→
    29→// ErrEmptyRepository is returned when the repository has no commits yet.
    30→var ErrEmptyRepository = errors.New("repository has no commits yet")
    31→
    32→// SessionIDConflictError is returned when trying to start a new session
    33→// but the shadow branch already has commits from a different session ID.
    34→// This prevents orphaning existing session work.
    35→type SessionIDConflictError struct {
    36→	ExistingSession string // Session ID found in the shadow branch
    37→	NewSession      string // Session ID being initialized
    38→	ShadowBranch    string // The shadow branch name (e.g., "entire/abc1234")
    39→}
    40→
    41→func (e *SessionIDConflictError) Error() string {
    42→	return "session ID conflict: shadow branch has commits from a different session"
    43→}
    44→
    45→// ExtractToolUseIDFromTaskMetadataDir extracts the ToolUseID from a task metadata directory path.
    46→// Task metadata dirs have format: .entire/metadata/<session>/tasks/<toolUseID>
    47→// Returns empty string if not a task metadata directory.
    48→func ExtractToolUseIDFromTaskMetadataDir(metadataDir string) string {
    49→	parts := strings.Split(metadataDir, "/")
    50→	if len(parts) >= 2 && parts[len(parts)-2] == "tasks" {
    51→		return parts[len(parts)-1]
    52→	}
    53→	return ""
    54→}
    55→
    56→// SessionInfo contains information about the current session state.
    57→// This is used to generate trailers for linking commits to their AI session.
    58→type SessionInfo struct {
    59→	// SessionID is the session identifier extracted from the latest commit's metadata
    60→	SessionID string
    61→
    62→	// Reference is a strategy-specific reference string.
    63→	// For manual-commit strategy: "entire/abc1234" (the shadow branch name)
    64→	// Empty for commit strategy (metadata is in the same commit).
    65→	Reference string
    66→
    67→	// CommitHash is the full SHA of the commit containing the session metadata.
    68→	// Empty for commit strategy.
    69→	CommitHash string
    70→}
    71→
    72→// RewindPoint represents a point to which the user can rewind.
    73→// This abstraction allows different strategies to use different
    74→// identifiers (commit hashes, branch names, stash refs, etc.)
    75→type RewindPoint struct {
    76→	// ID is the unique identifier for this rewind point
    77→	// (commit hash, branch name, stash ref, etc.)
    78→	ID string
    79→
    80→	// Message is the human-readable description/summary
    81→	Message string
    82→
    83→	// MetadataDir is the path to the metadata directory
    84→	MetadataDir string
    85→
    86→	// Date is when this rewind point was created
    87→	Date time.Time
    88→
    89→	// IsTaskCheckpoint indicates if this is a task checkpoint (vs a session checkpoint)
    90→	IsTaskCheckpoint bool
    91→
    92→	// ToolUseID is the tool use ID for task checkpoints (empty for session checkpoints)
    93→	ToolUseID string
    94→
    95→	// IsLogsOnly indicates this is a commit with session logs but no shadow branch state.
    96→	// The logs can be restored from entire/checkpoints/v1, but file state requires git checkout.
    97→	IsLogsOnly bool
    98→
    99→	// CheckpointID is the stable 12-hex-char identifier for logs-only points.
   100→	// Used to retrieve logs from entire/checkpoints/v1/<id[:2]>/<id[2:]>/full.jsonl
   101→	// Empty for shadow branch checkpoints (uncommitted).
   102→	CheckpointID id.CheckpointID
   103→
   104→	// Agent is the human-readable name of the agent that created this checkpoint
   105→	// (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor")
   106→	Agent agent.AgentType
   107→
   108→	// SessionID is the session identifier for this checkpoint.
   109→	// Used to distinguish checkpoints from different concurrent sessions.
   110→	SessionID string
   111→
   112→	// SessionPrompt is the initial prompt that started this session.
   113→	// Used to help users identify which session a checkpoint belongs to.
   114→	SessionPrompt string
   115→
   116→	// SessionCount is the number of sessions in this checkpoint (1 for single-session).
   117→	// Only populated for logs-only points with multi-session checkpoints.
   118→	SessionCount int
   119→
   120→	// SessionIDs contains all session IDs when this is a multi-session checkpoint.
   121→	// The last entry is the most recent session (same as SessionID).
   122→	// Only populated for logs-only points with multi-session checkpoints.
   123→	SessionIDs []string
   124→
   125→	// SessionPrompts contains the first prompt for each session (parallel to SessionIDs).
   126→	// Used to display context when showing resume commands for multi-session checkpoints.
   127→	SessionPrompts []string
   128→}
   129→
   130→// RewindPreview describes what will happen when rewinding to a checkpoint.
   131→// Used to warn users about files that will be modified or deleted.
   132→type RewindPreview struct {
   133→	// FilesToRestore are files from the checkpoint that will be written/restored.
   134→	FilesToRestore []string
   135→
   136→	// FilesToDelete are untracked files that will be removed.
   137→	// These are files created after the checkpoint that aren't in the checkpoint tree
   138→	// and weren't present at session start.
   139→	FilesToDelete []string
   140→
   141→	// TrackedChanges are tracked files with uncommitted changes that will be reverted.
   142→	// These come from the existing CanRewind() warning.
   143→	TrackedChanges []string
   144→}
   145→
   146→// StepContext contains all information needed for saving a step checkpoint.
   147→// All file paths should be pre-filtered and normalized by the CLI layer.
   148→type StepContext struct {
   149→	// SessionID is the Claude Code session identifier
   150→	SessionID string
   151→
   152→	// ModifiedFiles is the list of files modified during the session
   153→	// (extracted from the transcript, already filtered and relative)
   154→	ModifiedFiles []string
   155→
   156→	// NewFiles is the list of new files created during the session
   157→	// (pre-computed by CLI from pre-prompt state comparison)
   158→	NewFiles []string
   159→
   160→	// DeletedFiles is the list of files deleted during the session
   161→	// (tracked files that no longer exist)
   162→	DeletedFiles []string
   163→
   164→	// MetadataDir is the path to the session metadata directory
   165→	MetadataDir string
   166→
   167→	// MetadataDirAbs is the absolute path to the session metadata directory
   168→	MetadataDirAbs string
   169→
   170→	// CommitMessage is the generated commit message
   171→	CommitMessage string
   172→
   173→	// TranscriptPath is the path to the transcript file
   174→	TranscriptPath string
   175→
   176→	// AuthorName is the name to use for commits
   177→	AuthorName string
   178→
   179→	// AuthorEmail is the email to use for commits
   180→	AuthorEmail string
   181→
   182→	// AgentType is the human-readable agent name (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor")
   183→	AgentType agent.AgentType
   184→
   185→	// Transcript position at step/turn start - tracks what was added during this step
   186→	StepTranscriptIdentifier string // Last identifier when step started (UUID for Claude, message ID for Gemini)
   187→	StepTranscriptStart      int    // Transcript line count when this step/turn started
   188→
   189→	// TokenUsage contains the token usage for this checkpoint
   190→	TokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage
   191→}
   192→
   193→// TaskStepContext contains all information needed for saving a task step checkpoint.
   194→// This is called by the PostToolUse[Task] hook when a subagent completes.
   195→// The strategy is responsible for creating metadata structures and storing them
   196→// according to its storage approach.
   197→type TaskStepContext struct {
   198→	// SessionID is the Claude Code session identifier
   199→	SessionID string
   200→
   201→	// ToolUseID is the unique identifier for this Task tool invocation
   202→	ToolUseID string
   203→
   204→	// AgentID is the subagent identifier (from tool_response.agentId)
   205→	AgentID string
   206→
   207→	// ModifiedFiles is the list of files modified by the subagent
   208→	// (extracted from the subagent's transcript)
   209→	ModifiedFiles []string
   210→
   211→	// NewFiles is the list of new files created by the subagent
   212→	// (computed from pre-task state comparison)
   213→	NewFiles []string
   214→
   215→	// DeletedFiles is the list of files deleted by the subagent
   216→	DeletedFiles []string
   217→
   218→	// TranscriptPath is the path to the main session transcript
   219→	TranscriptPath string
   220→
   221→	// SubagentTranscriptPath is the path to the subagent's transcript (if available)
   222→	SubagentTranscriptPath string
   223→
   224→	// CheckpointUUID is the UUID for transcript truncation when rewinding
   225→	CheckpointUUID string
   226→
   227→	// AuthorName is the name to use for commits
   228→	AuthorName string
   229→
   230→	// AuthorEmail is the email to use for commits
   231→	AuthorEmail string
   232→
   233→	// IsIncremental indicates this is an incremental checkpoint during task execution,
   234→	// not a final task completion checkpoint. When true:
   235→	// - Writes to checkpoints/NNN-{tool-use-id}.json instead of checkpoint.json
   236→	// - Skips transcript handling
   237→	// - Uses incremental commit message
   238→	IsIncremental bool
   239→
   240→	// IncrementalSequence is the checkpoint sequence number (1, 2, 3, ...)
   241→	// Only used when IsIncremental is true
   242→	IncrementalSequence int
   243→
   244→	// IncrementalType is the tool that triggered this checkpoint ("TodoWrite", "Edit", etc.)
   245→	// Only used when IsIncremental is true
   246→	IncrementalType string
   247→
   248→	// IncrementalData is the tool_input payload for this checkpoint
   249→	// Only used when IsIncremental is true
   250→	IncrementalData json.RawMessage
   251→
   252→	// SubagentType is the type of subagent (e.g., "dev", "reviewer")
   253→	// Extracted from tool_input.subagent_type in Task tool
   254→	// Used for descriptive commit messages
   255→	SubagentType string
   256→
   257→	// TaskDescription is the task description provided to the subagent
   258→	// Extracted from tool_input.description in Task tool
   259→	// Used for descriptive commit messages
   260→	TaskDescription string
   261→
   262→	// TodoContent is the content of the in-progress todo item
   263→	// Extracted from tool_input.todos where status == "in_progress"
   264→	// Used for descriptive incremental checkpoint messages
   265→	TodoContent string
   266→
   267→	// AgentType is the human-readable agent name (e.g., "Claude Code", "Cursor")
   268→	AgentType agent.AgentType
   269→}
   270→
   271→// TaskCheckpoint contains the checkpoint information written to checkpoint.json
   272→type TaskCheckpoint struct {
   273→	SessionID      string `json:"session_id"`
   274→	ToolUseID      string `json:"tool_use_id"`
   275→	CheckpointUUID string `json:"checkpoint_uuid"`
   276→	AgentID        string `json:"agent_id,omitempty"`
   277→}
   278→
   279→// SubagentCheckpoint represents an intermediate checkpoint created during subagent execution.
   280→// These are created by PostToolUse hooks for tools like TodoWrite, Edit, Write.
   281→type SubagentCheckpoint struct {
   282→	Type      string          `json:"type"`        // Tool name: "TodoWrite", "Edit", "Write"
   283→	ToolUseID string          `json:"tool_use_id"` // The tool use ID that created this checkpoint
   284→	Timestamp time.Time       `json:"timestamp"`   // When the checkpoint was created
   285→	Data      json.RawMessage `json:"data"`        // Type-specific payload (tool_input)
   286→}
   287→
   288→// TaskMetadataDir returns the path to a task's metadata directory
   289→// within the session metadata directory.
   290→func TaskMetadataDir(sessionMetadataDir, toolUseID string) string {
   291→	return sessionMetadataDir + "/tasks/" + toolUseID
   292→}
   293→
   294→// ReadTaskCheckpoint reads the checkpoint.json file from a task metadata directory.
   295→// This is used during rewind to get the checkpoint UUID for transcript truncation.
   296→func ReadTaskCheckpoint(taskMetadataDir string) (*TaskCheckpoint, error) {
   297→	checkpointFile := taskMetadataDir + "/checkpoint.json"
   298→
   299→	data, err := os.ReadFile(checkpointFile) //nolint:gosec // Reading from controlled git metadata path
   300→	if err != nil {
   301→		return nil, err //nolint:wrapcheck // already present in codebase
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	var checkpoint TaskCheckpoint
   305→	if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &checkpoint); err != nil {
   306→		return nil, err //nolint:wrapcheck // already present in codebase
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	return &checkpoint, nil
   310→}
   311→
   312→// Strategy defines the interface for git operation strategies.
   313→// Different implementations can use commits, branches, stashes, etc.
   314→//
   315→// Note: State capture (tracking untracked files before a session) is handled
   316→// by the CLI layer, not the strategy. The strategy receives pre-computed
   317→// file lists in StepContext.
   318→type Strategy interface {
   319→	// Name returns the strategy identifier (e.g., "commit", "branch", "stash")
   320→	Name() string
   321→
   322→	// Description returns a human-readable description for the setup wizard
   323→	Description() string
   324→
   325→	// ValidateRepository checks if the repository is in a valid state
   326→	// for this strategy to operate. Returns an error if validation fails.
   327→	ValidateRepository() error
   328→
   329→	// SaveStep is called on Stop to save all session changes
   330→	// using this strategy's approach (commit, branch, stash, etc.)
   331→	SaveStep(ctx StepContext) error
   332→
   333→	// SaveTaskStep is called by PostToolUse[Task] hook when a subagent completes.
   334→	// Creates a checkpoint commit with task metadata for later rewind.
   335→	// Different strategies may handle this differently:
   336→	// - Commit strategy: commits to active branch
   337→	// - Manual-commit strategy: commits to shadow branch
   338→	// - Auto-commit strategy: commits logs to shadow only (code deferred to Stop)
   339→	SaveTaskStep(ctx TaskStepContext) error
   340→
   341→	// GetRewindPoints returns available points to rewind to.
   342→	// The limit parameter controls the maximum number of points to return.
   343→	GetRewindPoints(limit int) ([]RewindPoint, error)
   344→
   345→	// Rewind restores the repository to the given rewind point.
   346→	// The metadataDir in the point is used to restore the session transcript.
   347→	Rewind(point RewindPoint) error
   348→
   349→	// CanRewind checks if rewinding is currently possible.
   350→	// Returns (canRewind, reason if not, error)
   351→	CanRewind() (bool, string, error)
   352→
   353→	// PreviewRewind returns what will happen if rewinding to the given point.
   354→	// This allows showing warnings about files that will be deleted before the rewind.
   355→	// Returns nil if preview is not supported (e.g., auto-commit strategy).
   356→	PreviewRewind(point RewindPoint) (*RewindPreview, error)
   357→
   358→	// GetTaskCheckpoint returns the task checkpoint for a given rewind point.
   359→	// For strategies that store checkpoints in git (auto-commit), this reads from the branch.
   360→	// For strategies that store checkpoints on disk (commit, manual-commit), this reads from the filesystem.
   361→	// Returns nil, nil if not a task checkpoint or checkpoint not found.
   362→	GetTaskCheckpoint(point RewindPoint) (*TaskCheckpoint, error)
   363→
   364→	// GetTaskCheckpointTranscript returns the session transcript for a task checkpoint.
   365→	// For strategies that store transcripts in git (auto-commit), this reads from the branch.
   366→	// For strategies that store transcripts on disk (commit, manual-commit), this reads from the filesystem.
   367→	GetTaskCheckpointTranscript(point RewindPoint) ([]byte, error)
   368→
   369→	// GetSessionInfo returns session information for linking commits.
   370→	// This is used by the context command to generate trailers.
   371→	// Returns ErrNoSession if no session info is available.
   372→	GetSessionInfo() (*SessionInfo, error)
   373→
   374→	// EnsureSetup ensures the strategy's required setup is in place,
   375→	// installing any missing pieces (git hooks, gitignore entries, etc.).
   376→	// Returns nil if setup is complete or was successfully installed.
   377→	EnsureSetup() error
   378→
   379→	// NOTE: ListSessions and GetSession are standalone functions in session.go.
   380→	// They read from entire/checkpoints/v1 and merge with SessionSource if implemented.
   381→
   382→	// GetMetadataRef returns a reference to the metadata commit for the given checkpoint.
   383→	// Format: "<branch>@<commit-sha>" (e.g., "entire/checkpoints/v1@abc123").
   384→	// Returns empty string if not applicable (e.g., commit strategy with filesystem metadata).
   385→	GetMetadataRef(checkpoint Checkpoint) string
   386→
   387→	// GetSessionMetadataRef returns a reference to the most recent metadata commit for a session.
   388→	// Format: "<branch>@<commit-sha>" (e.g., "entire/checkpoints/v1@abc123").
   389→	// Returns empty string if not applicable or session not found.
   390→	GetSessionMetadataRef(sessionID string) string
   391→
   392→	// GetSessionContext returns the context.md content for a session.
   393→	// Returns empty string if not available.
   394→	GetSessionContext(sessionID string) string
   395→
   396→	// GetCheckpointLog returns the session transcript for a specific checkpoint.
   397→	// For strategies that store transcripts in git branches (auto-commit, manual-commit),
   398→	// this reads from the checkpoint's commit tree.
   399→	// For strategies that store on disk (commit), reads from the filesystem.
   400→	// Returns ErrNoMetadata if transcript is not available.
   401→	GetCheckpointLog(checkpoint Checkpoint) ([]byte, error)
   402→}
   403→
   404→// SessionInitializer is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   405→// initialize session state when a user prompt is submitted.
   406→// Strategies like manual-commit use this to create session state files that
   407→// the git prepare-commit-msg hook can detect.
   408→type SessionInitializer interface {
   409→	// InitializeSession creates session state for a new session.
   410→	// Called during UserPromptSubmit hook before any checkpoints are created.
   411→	// agentType is the human-readable name of the agent (e.g., "Claude Code").
   412→	// transcriptPath is the path to the live transcript file (for mid-session commit detection).
   413→	// userPrompt is the user's prompt text (stored truncated as FirstPrompt for display).
   414→	InitializeSession(sessionID string, agentType agent.AgentType, transcriptPath string, userPrompt string) error
   415→}
   416→
   417→// PrepareCommitMsgHandler is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   418→// handle the git prepare-commit-msg hook.
   419→type PrepareCommitMsgHandler interface {
   420→	// PrepareCommitMsg is called by the git prepare-commit-msg hook.
   421→	// It can modify the commit message file to add trailers, etc.
   422→	// The source parameter indicates how the commit was initiated:
   423→	//   - "" or "template": normal editor flow
   424→	//   - "message": using -m or -F flag
   425→	//   - "merge": merge commit
   426→	//   - "squash": squash merge
   427→	//   - "commit": amend with -c/-C
   428→	// Should return nil on errors to not block commits (log warnings to stderr).
   429→	PrepareCommitMsg(commitMsgFile string, source string) error
   430→}
   431→
   432→// PostCommitHandler is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   433→// handle the git post-commit hook.
   434→type PostCommitHandler interface {
   435→	// PostCommit is called by the git post-commit hook after a commit is created.
   436→	// Used to perform actions like condensing session data after commits.
   437→	// Should return nil on errors to not block subsequent operations (log warnings to stderr).
   438→	PostCommit() error
   439→}
   440→
   441→// CommitMsgHandler is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   442→// handle the git commit-msg hook.
   443→type CommitMsgHandler interface {
   444→	// CommitMsg is called by the git commit-msg hook after the user edits the message.
   445→	// Used to validate or modify the final commit message before the commit is created.
   446→	// If this returns an error, the commit is aborted.
   447→	CommitMsg(commitMsgFile string) error
   448→}
   449→
   450→// PrePushHandler is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   451→// handle the git pre-push hook.
   452→type PrePushHandler interface {
   453→	// PrePush is called by the git pre-push hook before pushing to a remote.
   454→	// Used to push session branches (e.g., entire/checkpoints/v1) alongside user pushes.
   455→	// The remote parameter is the name of the remote being pushed to.
   456→	// Should return nil on errors to not block pushes (log warnings to stderr).
   457→	PrePush(remote string) error
   458→}
   459→
   460→// TurnEndHandler is an optional interface for strategies that need to
   461→// perform work when an agent turn ends (ACTIVE → IDLE).
   462→// For example, manual-commit strategy uses this to finalize checkpoints
   463→// with the full session transcript.
   464→type TurnEndHandler interface {
   465→	// HandleTurnEnd performs strategy-specific cleanup at the end of a turn.
   466→	// Work items are read from state (e.g. TurnCheckpointIDs), not from the
   467→	// action list. The state has already been updated by ApplyTransition;
   468→	// the caller saves it after this method returns.
   469→	HandleTurnEnd(state *session.State) error
   470→}
   471→
   472→// RestoredSession describes a single session that was restored by RestoreLogsOnly.
   473→// Each session may come from a different agent, so callers use this to print
   474→// per-session resume commands without re-reading the metadata tree.
   475→type RestoredSession struct {
   476→	SessionID string
   477→	Agent     agent.AgentType
   478→	Prompt    string
   479→}
   480→
   481→// LogsOnlyRestorer is an optional interface for strategies that support
   482→// restoring session logs without file state restoration.
   483→// This is used for "logs-only" rewind points where only the session transcript
   484→// can be restored (file state requires git checkout).
   485→type LogsOnlyRestorer interface {
   486→	// RestoreLogsOnly restores session logs from a logs-only rewind point.
   487→	// Does not modify the working directory - only restores the transcript
   488→	// to the agent's session directory (determined per-session from checkpoint metadata).
   489→	// If force is false, prompts for confirmation when local logs have newer timestamps.
   490→	// Returns info about each restored session so callers can print correct resume commands.
   491→	RestoreLogsOnly(point RewindPoint, force bool) ([]RestoredSession, error)
   492→}
   493→
   494→// SessionResetter is an optional interface for strategies that support
   495→// resetting session state and shadow branches.
   496→// This is used by the "reset" command to clean up shadow branches
   497→// and session state when a user wants to start fresh.
   498→type SessionResetter interface {
   499→	// Reset deletes the shadow branch and session state for the current HEAD.
   500→	// Returns nil if there's nothing to reset (no shadow branch).
   501→	Reset() error
   502→
   503→	// ResetSession clears the state for a single session and cleans up
   504→	// the shadow branch if no other sessions reference it.
   505→	// File changes remain in the working directory.
   506→	ResetSession(sessionID string) error
   507→}
   508→
   509→// SessionCondenser is an optional interface for strategies that support
   510→// force-condensing a session. This is used by "entire doctor" to
   511→// salvage stuck sessions by condensing their data to permanent storage.
   512→type SessionCondenser interface {
   513→	// CondenseSessionByID force-condenses a session and cleans up.
   514→	// Generates a new checkpoint ID, condenses to entire/checkpoints/v1,
   515→	// updates the session state, and removes the shadow branch
   516→	// if no other active sessions need it.
   517→	CondenseSessionByID(sessionID string) error
   518→}
   519→
   520→// ConcurrentSessionChecker is an optional interface for strategies that support
   521→// counting concurrent sessions with uncommitted changes.
   522→// This is used by the SessionStart hook to show an informational message about
   523→// how many other active conversations will be included in the next commit.
   524→type ConcurrentSessionChecker interface {
   525→	// CountOtherActiveSessionsWithCheckpoints returns the number of other active sessions
   526→	// with uncommitted checkpoints on the same base commit.
   527→	// Returns 0, nil if no such sessions exist.
   528→	CountOtherActiveSessionsWithCheckpoints(currentSessionID string) (int, error)
   529→}
   530→
   531→// SessionSource is an optional interface for strategies that provide additional
   532→// sessions beyond those stored on the entire/checkpoints/v1 branch.
   533→// For example, manual-commit strategy provides active sessions from .git/entire-sessions/
   534→// that haven't yet been condensed to entire/checkpoints/v1.
   535→//
   536→// ListSessions() automatically discovers all registered strategies, checks if they
   537→// implement SessionSource, and merges their additional sessions by ID.
   538→type SessionSource interface {
   539→	// GetAdditionalSessions returns sessions not yet on entire/checkpoints/v1 branch.
   540→	GetAdditionalSessions() ([]*Session, error)
   541→}
   542→
   543→// OrphanedItemsLister is an optional interface for strategies that can identify
   544→// orphaned items (shadow branches, session states, checkpoints) that should be
   545→// cleaned up. This is used by the "entire session cleanup" command.
   546→//
   547→// ListAllCleanupItems() automatically discovers all registered strategies, checks
   548→// if they implement OrphanedItemsLister, and combines their orphaned items.
   549→type OrphanedItemsLister interface {
   550→	// ListOrphanedItems returns items created by this strategy that are now orphaned.
   551→	// Each strategy defines what "orphaned" means for its own data structures.
   552→	ListOrphanedItems() ([]CleanupItem, error)
   553→}
   554→
